acceptance_criteria: Backend deployment posture is expressed as wasmCloud on Wasmtime with deployable component metadata instead of native service binaries.
The OS shell subset is rehomed under ui/ and exposes only the governed release apps for this product profile.
SurrealDB access is centralized behind shared repository abstractions and UI data access stays on typed SDK/contracts.
Workspace tooling, CI, and process validation encode the new ui and wasmCloud-first rules.

open_questions.question: Nomad host/service topology can be refined further once real lattice rollout automation is introduced.

objective: Translate the target architecture into repository-level requirements.
functional_requirements: Add ui/ as the authoritative Leptos/Tauri module and rehome the shell-first OS subset into it.
Expose wasmCloud component metadata, lattice rollout configuration, and WIT definitions for deployable workloads.
Provide a typed platform SDK for UI commands, queries, and event streams plus a shared SurrealDB repository layer.
non_functional_requirements: Keep contract boundaries versioned and additive-first.
Support browser/Tauri dual-target UI development without direct database coupling.
out_of_scope: Full production lattice rollout automation beyond manifest-level integration.
Rehoming every sample OS app into the product release profile.
success_metrics: Targeted Rust checks pass for platform, ui, workflow, and service integration crates.
Only Control Center, Terminal, and Settings remain in the shipped release registry.
assumptions: ../os is copied into ui/ rather than linked as a submodule or runtime dependency.
